summary: FiiLTHY.AI Phase 1 backend regression (Stripe subs + SendGrid + Admin + Referrals). 28/30 tests pass. 2 FAIL — both are the same root cause: stripe-python StripeObject no longer subclasses dict, so calling .get(...) on Session / event.data.object raises AttributeError and returns HTTP 500. Breaks (a) GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} after Stripe retrieves a real live session, and (b) POST /api/webhook/stripe on every valid event (signature verifies correctly, but handler then crashes on meta.get(...)). Admin (ban/unban/audit-log/announcements/feature-flags/broadcast/system-health), referral attribution + leaderboard + resolve, signup with referral_code, /auth/me new fields, banned-login 403, download PDF/bundle viral branding, plans w/ real live price_ids, create-checkout returns live checkout.stripe.com URL, payment_transactions mode=subscription w/ price_id — all PASS.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error. File /app/backend/routers/billing.py line 280-281 uses obj.get('metadata') / meta.get('filthy_user_id'). In current stripe SDK (1.x+), StripeObject does NOT inherit from dict and has no .get method — AttributeError: get. Signature verifies fine; duplicate-dedupe also works (inserted stripe_events row before the crash, so 2nd call returns 200 duplicate:true) but the FIRST call never updates user.plan/subscription_status, so real subscription payments silently fail to upgrade users. All webhook event types (checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.*) share the same obj.get / metadata access pattern and will crash.

500 Internal Server Error whenever Stripe successfully returns a live Session. File /app/backend/routers/billing.py line 108 does s.get('status') on a StripeObject which has no .get — AttributeError: get. Affects every real checkout-return page call. Fallback branch (res.ok=False) still works, so if Stripe is unreachable the endpoint returns a pending-state dict, but the success path is broken.

minor.issue: When STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is missing it silently falls back to parsing the payload without signature check (stripe.Event.construct_from). Should reject with 400/503 instead of accepting unsigned events in production.

action_items: Fix StripeObject .get(...) usage across routers/billing.py. Replace `obj.get('metadata')` / `s.get('status')` etc. with attribute access (obj.metadata, s.status, s.payment_status, s.subscription, s.customer, s.amount_total, s.currency). Safest pattern: `d = obj.to_dict_recursive() if hasattr(obj,'to_dict_recursive') else obj; d.get('metadata') or {}` — then treat d as a plain dict throughout both the webhook handler and checkout_status.

After fix, rerun /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_backend.py — the 2 failing tests (test_billing_status_returns_dict and test_webhook_checkout_session_completed_updates_user) should pass. The dedupe test inside the same test function will then also be meaningful.
Add explicit Stripe SDK version pin in requirements.txt and document the dict-migration (StripeObject no longer subclasses dict as of stripe-python 9.x / 11.x).
Consider failing hard when STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset in production rather than silently accepting unsigned events.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py still 1418 lines — continuing tech debt; auth/products/launch/tracking/tiktok/meta should be split into routers the same way billing/admin/referrals were.
routers/billing.py:280-281 — the `if hasattr(meta, 'get') else meta.get(...)` branch is unreachable (both sides call .get) and hides the real bug.
routers/billing.py startup dependency: if ensure_prices() fails, /api/billing/plans returns empty price_ids silently. Consider health-gating the endpoint.
services/stripe_service.verify_webhook has an insecure fallback when STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is missing (parses event without signature). Production should hard-fail.
routers/admin.py:258 broadcast_email iterates users serially inside the request loop — blocks event loop for large lists. Push to background task.

services/email.py returns {ok:false, mocked:true} when SendGrid is unconfigured — good; but admin broadcast counts that as `failed` in non-test mode. Consider distinguishing `mocked` vs `failed` in the response.
services/referrals.py _gen_code uses only first 4 chars of name — collisions possible at scale; while-loop does retry so it's safe, but consider longer tail.
test_credentials: Test user test1@filthy.ai / test123 per /app/memory/test_credentials.md exists but password unknown in this env — used fresh per-run signups instead. OWNER_EMAIL (stackdigitz@gmail.com) was deleted + re-signed up inside the owner_user fixture to guarantee admin role; password is 'ownerpass123' for this run only.
seed_data_creation: Per run creates: 1 plain test user, 1 referral-attributed user, 1 generated product (LLM), 1 checkout session (unused Stripe test live session id), and the OWNER admin account (deleted + re-signed on each run). Test announcement + feature flag are created and announcement is deleted by test; feature-flag TEST_FLAG persists (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_backend.py. Run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_phase1_backend.py -v. The 2 known failures both trace to stripe-python StripeObject.get() AttributeError — they will stay RED until routers/billing.py is fixed to use attribute access or obj.to_dict_recursive(). Existing regression suite /app/backend/tests/test_filthy_backend.py (iteration_1) is still valid and independent. Note: owner_user fixture deletes any pre-existing stackdigitz@gmail.com doc to guarantee admin role — if another test depends on a stable owner account, coordinate ordering.
rca of the issue: stripe-python SDK: in recent versions (>=8.x) the StripeObject class no longer inherits from `dict`, so `obj.get('field')` calls __getattr__ for the attribute `get`, which doesn't exist on StripeObject → AttributeError propagates as HTTP 500. Code was written assuming StripeObject acts like a dict. Reproduction: POST a valid signed event to /api/webhook/stripe → 500 (but stripe_events row IS inserted before the crash, so duplicate detection on the 2nd call spuriously returns received:true,duplicate:true). Mitigation: in /app/backend/routers/billing.py convert `obj`/`s` to a plain dict once at the top of each handler (e.g. `d = obj.to_dict_recursive() if hasattr(obj,'to_dict_recursive') else dict(obj)`) and use d.get(...) thereafter. For the webhook’s defensive programming, also guard meta.get — or just use `meta = d.get('metadata') or {}` where d is the dict conversion.
